---
title: "Emily Taylor"
---

## Emily Taylor

**Dates:** (1795-1872)

### Description

contributor to <i>MP</i> (1859-1869), sometimes as 'T.'

### Biography

A school teacher, partly deaf, she was born a Unitarian, and joined the Church of England under the influence of F. D. Maurice. See ‘New Year’s Words from the Editor’  <i>MP</i> 3s 11 (January 1886), 41: ‘Long ago, in the earlier agest of the ‘Monthly Packet,’ a New Year’s address used to come with unfailing briskness, force, and truth, from the pen of Miss Emily Taylor, then known to us only by her initial T.’

### Letters

- Letter from Novr 22d 1864
- Letter from Novr 8th 1864
- Letter from March 18th 1861
- Letter from Ash Wednesday [9 March 1859] 
- Letter from Decr 9th [1856]
- Letter from Decr 5th [1856]
- Letter from Decr 3d [1856]

---

[← Back to all letters](../letters.html)
